title: A smell that reminds me of my father
summary: Describe a smell that always brings back a memory of your father.
topic: A smell that reminds me of my father
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a smell that reminds you of your father (or another older relative). Say what the smell is, where you meet it, and the memory it brings. Use a mix of present and past simple.
hintStarters: When I walk past a small shop in Marrakech, I…
The smell of fresh mint and tea always…
sampleText: When I walk past a small shop in Marrakech, I sometimes stop and breathe. The shop sells fresh mint and green tea. The smell is sweet and a little sharp. This smell always reminds me of my father. He made mint tea every morning in our small kitchen. He put a lot of leaves in a silver pot. He poured the tea from very high above the small cups. He said the bubbles made the tea taste better. I sat at the wooden table in my school clothes. He gave me a small cup with a lot of sugar. We did not speak much in the morning. We just drank tea and we looked out of the window. My father died last year. Now I live in a different city. But every time I smell mint tea on the street, I am six years old again. I am in my father's kitchen. I can almost hear the sound of the silver pot on the table. A smell is a small door to a big memory.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: bạc hà tươi và trà xanh
gợi cho tôi nhớ về bố

sampleAnnotations.phrase: poured the tea from very high
the bubbles made the tea taste better

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trong bộ đồng phục đi học

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a small door to a big memory
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cánh cửa nhỏ vào một ký ức lớn

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'remind someone of something'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'remind + tân ngữ + of + danh từ' nghĩa là 'làm ai đó nhớ về điều gì': 'This smell reminds me of my father'. Không nói 'reminds me about'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The pattern is 'remind + object + of + noun': 'This smell reminds me of my father'. Do not say 'reminds me about'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for general truths, past simple for memories
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả thói quen hiện tại, dùng hiện tại đơn: 'I walk past', 'I smell'. Khi nhớ về quá khứ, dùng quá khứ đơn: 'He made', 'He poured', 'I sat'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: For current habits, use present simple: 'I walk past', 'I smell'. For past memories, use past simple: 'He made', 'He poured', 'I sat'.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi đi ngang một tiệm nhỏ ở Marrakech, đôi khi tôi dừng lại và hít thở. Tiệm bán bạc hà tươi và trà xanh. Mùi vừa ngọt vừa hơi gắt. Mùi này luôn làm tôi nhớ về bố. Bố tôi pha trà bạc hà mỗi sáng trong căn bếp nhỏ. Ông cho rất nhiều lá vào ấm bạc. Ông rót trà từ trên rất cao vào những tách nhỏ. Ông nói bọt làm trà ngon hơn. Tôi ngồi ở bàn gỗ trong bộ đồng phục đi học. Ông đưa tôi một tách nhỏ với nhiều đường. Buổi sáng chúng tôi không nói chuyện nhiều. Chúng tôi chỉ uống trà và nhìn ra cửa sổ. Bố tôi mất năm ngoái. Bây giờ tôi sống ở thành phố khác. Nhưng mỗi khi ngửi mùi trà bạc hà trên phố, tôi lại là đứa trẻ sáu tuổi. Tôi đang ở trong bếp của bố. Tôi gần như nghe được tiếng ấm bạc đặt trên bàn. Một mùi hương là cánh cửa nhỏ vào một ký ức lớn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where does the writer live now (when smelling the mint)?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'When I walk past a small shop in Marrakech'.
The text says 'The shop sells fresh mint and green tea'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who used to make mint tea every morning?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He made mint tea every morning' — referring to the father.
mcQuestions.hint: The father is the focus of the memory.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did the father pour the tea?
options: Very slowly close to the cup
From very high above the small cups
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He poured the tea from very high above the small cups'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer and his father do in the morning?
options: They drank tea and looked out of the window.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'We just drank tea and we looked out of the window'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the activity after 'we did not speak much'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which preposition is correct: 'The smell reminds me ___ my father.'
mcQuestions.explanation: The correct pattern is 'remind someone of something'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple of 'make'.
Which sentence is in the present simple?
options: He poured the tea from very high.
I walked past the shop yesterday.
He gave me a small cup.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I sometimes stop and breathe' is present simple, describing a current habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the form without -ed and not in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why did the father say the tea tasted better?
options: Because of the bubbles when he poured it from high above
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He said the bubbles made the tea taste better'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer mean by 'A smell is a small door to a big memory'?
options: Smells can bring back strong memories from the past.
mcQuestions.explanation: A smell triggers a powerful memory, like opening a door into the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about how the mint smell takes him back to childhood.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do smells often bring back memories more strongly than photographs or words?
